page: Amazon Web Services c8g.medium is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 processor. It features 1 dedicated physical core (1 vCPU) running at 2.8 GHz, with 2.0 GB of system memory. The processor includes 64 KB of L1D cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, alongside hardware-accelerated AES cryptography. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a distinct profile for single-threaded versus multi-threaded execution. In single-core stress-ng testing, the server achieves an average score of 1693.68, matching its multi-core score due to the single-vCPU limitation. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression performs strongly at 14.02 GB/sec, and gzip compression registers an average throughput of 30.01 GB/sec. However, memory bandwidth is constrained, with large-block write speeds at 11.61 GB/sec and read speeds at 30.05 GB/sec.

The c8g.medium offers a highly cost-efficient entry point into the Graviton8 ARM64 ecosystem, providing dedicated compute resources without the CPU credit limitations of burstable instances. However, the primary tradeoff is the strict resource ceiling of 1 vCPU and 2.0 GB of RAM, which limits its suitability for parallel processing, large-scale database operations, or memory-intensive tasks. This instance is best suited for lightweight single-threaded workloads, basic web serving, microservices, and development or testing environments optimized for ARM64 architecture.
description: The Amazon Web Services c8g.medium is a compute-optimized instance featuring a single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 core running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 2.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides a 1 Gbps baseline network speed and lacks local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmarks show average single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression, though memory bandwidth and multi-threaded capabilities are limited by its single-vCPU configuration. This instance offers a cost-effective entry point for ARM64 development, lightweight single-threaded tasks, microservices, and basic web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.medium is a compute-optimized ARM64 server featuring 1 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 core, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for lightweight single-threaded workloads.

tagline: A dedicated single-core ARM64 compute instance designed for lightweight single-threaded workloads, microservices, and cost-efficient development environments.

1 vCPU paired with 2.0 GB of system memory
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for lightweight microservices, basic web serving, and ARM64 development
